description: Unsolved problems in analysis, PDEs, and dynamical systems.

facts.context: For 3D Navier-Stokes, energy inequality and weak solutions are known. The gap is:
facts.truth: whether weak solutions are unique and regular (smooth)
distractors: whether energy is conserved in the inviscid limit
whether weak solutions exist for arbitrary initial data
whether pressure can be uniquely recovered from velocity

facts.context: For 3D incompressible Euler equations, finite-time blowup:
facts.truth: is conjectured but unproven; Elgindi showed blowup for C^(1,α) data
distractors: has been ruled out by Beale-Kato-Majda criterion application
is proven to occur for all smooth compactly supported data
is irrelevant because 3D Euler has global weak solutions

facts.context: The Kakeya conjecture states that Kakeya sets (containing unit segment in each direction) have:
facts.truth: Hausdorff dimension n in Rⁿ; proven for n=2, open for n≥3
distractors: Hausdorff dimension n-1 in Rⁿ; proven for all dimensions
Hausdorff dimension (n+1)/2 by Wolff's bound in all cases
measure zero but full Hausdorff dimension in all Rⁿ proven

facts.context: The Erdős distinct distances problem asks: n points in the plane determine how many distinct distances?
facts.truth: at least cn/√(log n) proven by Guth-Katz; conjectured tight
distractors: at least cn^(2/3) proven by Solymosi-Tóth; conjectured tight
exactly Θ(n) proven by Szemerédi-Trotter; now fully resolved
at least cn^(4/5) proven by Katz-Tardos; conjectured cn/log n

facts.context: The local-global conjecture for elliptic curves concerns:
facts.truth: whether local solvability over all completions implies global solvability
distractors: whether the Tate-Shafarevich group is always finite for all curves
whether rational points can be computed in polynomial time always
whether the rank of the Mordell-Weil group is always bounded

facts.context: The Erdős-Turán conjecture on additive bases states:
facts.truth: if Σ1/aₙ diverges, the sequence contains arbitrarily long APs
distractors: if Σ1/aₙ converges, the sequence is a Sidon set necessarily
if Σ1/aₙ diverges, the sequence has positive upper density
if Σ1/aₙ diverges, the sequence is an asymptotic basis of order 2

facts.context: The corners theorem (Ajtai-Szemerédi) proves that dense subsets of [n]² contain:
distractors: squares: quadruples (x,y), (x+d,y), (x,y+d), (x+d,y+d)
lines: triples (x,y), (x+d,y+d), (x+2d,y+2d) for some d
rectangles: (x,y), (x+a,y), (x,y+b), (x+a,y+b) for a,b>0

facts.context: The Sarnak conjecture on the Möbius function states that:
facts.truth: μ(n) is orthogonal to all deterministic sequences (zero entropy)
distractors: μ(n) correlates with the Liouville function for all intervals
μ(n) has bounded partial sums along arithmetic progressions
μ(n) is equidistributed among {-1,0,1} with equal frequency

facts.context: Singmaster's conjecture states that how many times a number >1 appears in Pascal's triangle is:
distractors: unbounded; proven by Luca for numbers of form C(2n,n)
exactly 4; proven for all sufficiently large entries by Kane
at most log(n); proven by exponential growth of binomials

facts.context: The Furstenberg conjecture on ×2, ×3 ergodicity states:
facts.truth: the only ergodic ×2 and ×3 invariant measure on R/Z is Lebesgue
distractors: every ×2 invariant measure is also ×3 invariant automatically
the only ×2 ergodic measure on R/Z is the Dirac at zero
there exist uncountably many jointly ergodic ×2,×3 measures

facts.context: For minimal surfaces in Rⁿ, interior regularity holds for n≤7. For n≥8:
distractors: all minimal hypersurfaces remain smooth (Schoen-Simon theorem)
singularities have codimension at most 2 (Federer's theorem)
the singular set has Hausdorff dimension exactly n-8 always

facts.context: The uniform boundedness of partial sums of Fourier series for C¹ functions:
facts.truth: is proven; remains open for Hölder-continuous C^α with α<1
distractors: is proven for all continuous functions by Carleson's theorem
fails even for C¹; du Bois-Reymond gave a counterexample
is equivalent to the Riemann hypothesis for Dirichlet L-functions
